audit.title: Audit
audit.heading: Audit logs
tools.title: Tools
tools.heading: Firewall
tools.description: Check whether the system firewall allows inbound PalServer traffic.
tools.unsupported: No supported firewall command is available on this system.
tools.manual_block: A matching Block rule requires manual removal: {rules}
tools.fix_prompt: The firewall is blocking PalServer. Fix it now? Administrator approval is required.
tools.root_password_description: Firewall access requires administrator privileges. Enter the root password to continue.
tools.root_password_required: A root password is required to repair the firewall.
tools.migration_description: After importing a single-player or co-op world, let the player create a new character on the dedicated server, then swap the old character onto that new server ID. The server must be stopped before starting the migration.
tools.migration_confirm: This changes Level.sav and swaps {old} with {new}. A safety backup is created first. Continue?
tools.migration_needs_two: At least two player saves are required: the original save and the new character created by the server.
tools.migration_failed: Could not migrate the player ID: {error}
tools.migration_working: Migrating player ID and rewriting the save...
tools.migration_server_running: Stop the server before migrating player IDs.
tools.name_cache_confirm: This reads Level.sav and creates an ID-to-name cache for the player selectors. Continue?
tools.name_cache_failed: Could not build the player name cache: {error}
tools.name_cache_completed: Player name cache built for {count} players: {path}
mods.title: Mods
mods.compatibility_warning: Palworld updates can temporarily break UE4SS and its mods. If the server stops starting after an update, install a compatible UE4SS release or remove UE4SS before trying again.
mods.ue4ss_description: UE4SS provides the runtime used by Lua and Blueprint mods. Changes take effect the next time the server starts.
mods.server_not_installed: Install the Palworld server before managing UE4SS.
mods.native_linux_unsupported: UE4SS Lua/C++ management is not supported for native Linux Palworld servers.
mods.stop_server: Stop the Palworld server before installing, changing, or removing UE4SS.
mods.remove_warning: This removes the UE4SS loader. Lua mods are preserved in its Mods folder. Pak mods are not removed. This cannot be undone.
mods.no_lua: No Lua mods found. Add mod folders through the operating system folder.
mods.no_pak: No Pak mods found. Add .pak files through the operating system folder; Blueprint mods belong in LogicMods.
mods.open_failed: Could not open the mod folder: {error}
mods.pak_toggle_failed: Could not change the Pak mod state: {error}
mods.delete_failed: Could not delete the Pak mod: {error}
home.description: Palsitter is a free open source game server babysitter.
home.empty_message: Create a Palworld server or a placeholder for another game to get started.
home.unsupported_message: This game is currently a storage and navigation placeholder.
updater.author: Author
utils.shutdown_warning: Palsitter will save and gracefully stop every active server before closing.
utils.shutdown_details: The shutdown dialog cannot be dismissed. Continue?
utils.shutdown_stopping: Saving state and stopping active instances...
utils.shutdown_finished: Palsitter stopped, you can now safely close this window
utils.restart_warning: Palsitter will save every active managed server, detach its supervisor, and keep the server process running while the GUI restarts.
utils.restart_details: The GUI will briefly disconnect. Managed and externally managed server processes remain running and will be reattached after restart. Continue?
utils.restart_result_failed: Restart finished with errors. Review the results below.
utils.restart_disconnect_notice: The browser connection will drop briefly; refresh or reconnect to continue viewing progress.
utils.restart_external_detached: External server left running; watcher detached
utils.restart_external_detach_failed: Could not detach the external server watcher
utils.restart_handed_off: Server supervisor detached; server remains running
scheduler.title: Scheduler
scheduler.server_not_installed: Please start and install the server once
scheduler.open_failed: Could not open the PalServer folder: {error}
scheduler.low_disk_warning: Palsitter is running on a disk with available space below 10 GB. Errors and data loss might occur. Continue?

players.refresh_failed: Could not refresh all player data: {error}
players.unavailable: PalServer is not running or its REST API is unavailable.
players.name: Name

log.title: Log
console.hint.announce: Send a message to all players
console.hint.ban: Ban a player from the server
console.hint.kick: Kick a player from the server
console.hint.shutdown: Shut down the server after a delay
action.backup_created_stale: Backup created: {name}. The REST save failed ({error}), so the archive may contain an older on-disk state.
action.backup_skipped: Backup skipped: no save files found.
settings.title: Settings
settings.search_placeholder: Search setting labels or raw keys
settings.launch_docs: New profiles enable -useperfthreads and -UseMultithreadForDS, leave -NoAsyncLoadingThread off, and use one fewer worker thread than the detected logical CPU count.
settings.skip_backup_when_no_players: Skip backup when no player is online
settings.steamcmd_download_source: Palsitter downloads SteamCMD from Valve's SteamCMD installer URL documented on the Valve Developer Community SteamCMD page.
settings.help.server_name: Server Settings no longer stores a separate server name. Palsitter uses the profile name; the in-game server name lives in World Settings.
settings.help.executable: File path used as the first server start command argument. Its containing folder is used as the working directory for SteamCMD update and server start.
settings.help.dedicated_server_name: Stable 32-character uppercase letter/digit ID used for this server's save folder and GameUserSettings.ini.
settings.help.executable_args: One argument per line. Blank lines are ignored. These arguments are passed before the -port and -queryport flags when starting the server.
settings.help.launch_useperfthreads: Adds -useperfthreads. On by default for new profiles.
settings.help.launch_no_async_loading_th: Adds -NoAsyncLoadingThread. Off by default for new profiles.
settings.help.launch_use_multithread_for: Adds -UseMultithreadForDS. On by default for new profiles.
settings.help.launch_worker_threads_serv: Adds -NumberOfWorkerThreadsServer=<value>. New profiles default to the detected logical CPU count minus one, with a minimum of 1.
settings.help.launch_enable_gamedata_api: Adds -enable-gamedata-api to the PalServer launch command, enabling the REST /game-data world actor snapshot endpoint. On by default.
settings.help.launch_public_lobby: Adds -publiclobby so the server can appear in the community-server list.
settings.help.launch_log_format: Adds -logformat. Leave off to use PalServer's default logging behavior.
settings.help.extra_args: Argument list for additional launch options. Each row is one argument; structured options are shown above and cannot be duplicated here.
settings.help.query_port: Saved as query_port and passed to PalServer as -queryport=<value> when starting.
settings.help.update_on_start: When On, an installed server runs SteamCMD app_update before each managed start. Missing installations are always installed.
settings.help.auto_update: When On with Update on start, Palsitter checks Steam's public Palworld build every 30 minutes and restarts after the configured idle period when an update is available.
settings.help.auto_update_idle_minutes: Continuous number of minutes with zero verified online players before an available update starts the existing restart countdown. REST failures reset the timer.
settings.help.steam_validate: When On, appends validate to the SteamCMD app_update command. Off by default.
settings.help.steamcmd: Fixed under this profile's steamcmd folder. If it exists, Show opens that folder; otherwise Download fetches and extracts Valve's SteamCMD installer.
settings.help.backup_source: Fixed to the PalServer SaveGames/0 folder under this profile's SteamCMD install. Only child folders whose names contain digits or uppercase letters are included.
settings.help.backup_dir: Folder where Palsitter writes timestamped .zip backups and looks for backups to restore. New profiles default to profile/<name>/backups.
settings.help.backup_interval_minutes: Minutes the scheduled backup loop waits before trying the next backup. Zero disables scheduled backups.
settings.help.backup_retention_count: Maximum number of .zip backups kept in the backup folder. Older backups are deleted after a backup is created.
settings.help.skip_backup_when_no_player: When On, scheduled backups are skipped when the REST metrics report zero online players. Manual backups still run.
settings.help.memory_restart_percent: When observed system memory percent is above this value, Palsitter starts the memory-restart countdown.
settings.help.memory_restart_mb: Restarts only after PalServer and its child processes exceed this RSS amount for three consecutive samples. Zero disables the policy.
settings.help.memory_restart_countdown_m: Number of monitor ticks announced before a memory restart. Each tick decrements the value by one.
settings.help.restart_on_crash: When On, an unexpected server process exit is restarted. When Off, Palsitter marks the server warning instead.
settings.help.self_heal_enabled: When On, the configured number of crashes inside the trigger frame restores a backup from before that frame, then restarts. It is disabled when Restart on crash is Off.
settings.help.self_heal_trigger_frame_mi: Rolling window used to count crashes for self-heal. The rollback target must be older than the start of this frame.
settings.help.self_heal_trigger_crash_ti: Number of unexpected exits inside the trigger frame that activates self-heal.
settings.help.crash_restart_limit_per_ho: Maximum unexpected-exit restarts in a rolling hour. Reaching the limit leaves the instance in Warning until manually started.
settings.help.planned_restart_mode: Off disables planned restarts; Interval counts from supervisor start; Daily uses the host's local time.
settings.help.planned_restart_interval_h: Hours between planned restarts when Interval is selected.
settings.help.planned_restart_daily_time: Host-local 24-hour time used when Daily is selected.
settings.help.planned_restart_countdown_: Minutes of announcements before Palsitter saves and gracefully performs a planned restart.
reliability.title: Reliability history
reliability.external_skip: Planned restarts are skipped while this server is externally managed.
reliability.no_events: No lifecycle or restart events in this GUI session.
auto_restart.title: Auto Restart
auto_restart.description: Configure automatic crash recovery, process-memory restarts, and planned restarts.
restart_history.title: Restart History
restart_history.limitations: Detected causes are OS-reported termination types, not definitive root causes. Finding the faulty module, mod, function, or address requires a crash dump or application log.
restart_history.empty: No automatic restart decisions have been recorded.
restart_history.load_failed: Restart history could not be read: {error}
restart_history.memory_detail: Observed RSS {observed} MiB exceeded {threshold} MiB for {samples} consecutive samples.
restart_history.cause.stack_buffer_overr: Stack buffer overrun or fail-fast termination
restart_history.cause.forcibly_killed: Forcibly killed; the OS did not report whether this was out of memory
restart_history.cause.file_not_found: PalServer executable or working directory not found
settings.delete_warning: This removes only the profile reference. Server files, save games, and backups on disk are not deleted.
settings.delete_wipe_warning: This permanently deletes the instance's server files, save games, backups, and profile data. This cannot be undone.
settings.browse_limited: Showing {shown} of {total} entries. Use filter to narrow results.
settings.browse_error_invalid: Enter a valid server path. Relative paths resolve from Palsitter's working directory.
settings.browse_error_not_directory: That path is not a directory.
settings.browse_error_outside_base: That path is outside the allowed folder.
settings.browse_error_busy: The file picker is busy. Please try again.
settings.browse_error_selection_missing: The selected item no longer exists. Refresh and try again.
settings.browse_error_select_folder: Select a folder, or clear the selection to use the current folder.
world.title: World Settings
world.recovery_ini_detail: Palsitter can preserve a timestamped copy of the malformed file and regenerate managed defaults.
world.recovery_stop_required: Stop the server before changing a world configuration file.
world.recovery_confirm: Proceed with “{action}”? The original file will be retained as a timestamped copy.
backups.title: Saves & Backups
backups.open_failed: Could not open the backup folder: {error}
backups.rollback_confirm: Restore save data from {file}? A safety backup is mandatory. Only a server previously owned and running under Palsitter will restart afterward.
backups.backup_current: Back up the current save first
backups.safety_required: Palsitter must create a safety backup before restoring. The restore is cancelled if that backup fails.
backups.no_worlds: No managed dedicated worlds were found.
backups.switch_confirm: Switch the active world to {world}? The instance must be inactive and a safety backup will be created first.
backups.switch_complete: Active world changed to {world}; safety backup: {backup}
form.unsaved_message: You have unsaved changes. Do you want to save them before leaving?
validation.positive_integer: Enter a whole number greater than zero.
validation.nonnegative_integer: Enter zero or a positive whole number.
validation.positive_number: Enter a number greater than zero.
validation.time_hhmm: Enter a 24-hour time in HH:MM format.
validation.path_not_dir: That path must be a folder.
validation.path_not_file: That path must be a file.
validation.dedicated_server_name: Enter exactly 32 uppercase letters or digits.
world.field.enable_defense_other_guild_p: Enable base defense against other guilds
world.field.can_pickup_other_guild_death: Allow pickup of other guilds’ death drops
world.field.additional_drop_item_num_whe: Additional drop count on PvP kill
world.field.additional_drop_item_when_pl: Additional drop item type on PvP kill
world.field.display_pvp_item_num_on_worl: Show player drop counts on world map
Show base camp drop counts on world map
world.field.invisible_other_guild_base_c: Hide other guilds’ base area effect
world.field.enable_fast_travel_only_base: Restrict fast travel to base camps
world.field.exist_player_after_logout: Keep player in world after logout
world.field_help.difficulty: Preset difficulty package (None/Casual/Normal/Hard) that historically bundled several rate multipliers together - Casual eases capture/resource rates with no death penalty, Normal is the balanced default, Hard raises incoming damage/scarcity and intensifies the death penalty. On modern dedicated servers this preset is widely reported as non-functional; leave it at None and set individual rate fields directly.
world.field_help.randomizer_type: Controls whether wild Pal species/levels are shuffled at world generation. None: normal, unrandomized spawns. Region: shuffles which Pals appear but keeps each map region's original level range. All: fully randomizes Pals and levels across the whole map with no regional constraints (can put high-level Pals near the start).
world.field_help.randomizer_seed: Text seed that makes Pal randomization deterministic and repeatable - the same seed always produces the same randomized Pal placements. Leave blank to auto-generate a seed. Only applies at world generation; changing it later requires a world reset.
world.field_help.is_randomizer_pal_level: When On, wild Pal levels are fully randomized regardless of area. When Off, Pal levels stay within each area's normal level range even if RandomizerType shuffles which Pals spawn there.
world.field_help.day_time_speed_rate: Multiplier for how fast in-game time passes during the day. 1.0 is normal; values above 1 make daytime pass faster (shorter days), values below 1 make it pass slower (longer days).
world.field_help.night_time_speed_rate: Multiplier for how fast in-game time passes at night. 1.0 is normal; values above 1 make nighttime pass faster (shorter nights), values below 1 make it pass slower (longer nights).
world.field_help.exp_rate: Multiplier for experience points earned by players and Pals. 1.0 is normal; 2.0 doubles XP gain, 0.5 halves it.
world.field_help.pal_capture_rate: Multiplier for the chance a Pal Sphere successfully captures a Pal. 1.0 is normal; higher values make Pals easier to catch, lower values make capture harder.
world.field_help.pal_spawn_num_rate: Multiplier for how many wild Pals populate the map at once. 1.0 is normal density; higher values spawn more Pals but can increase server load, lower values spawn fewer.
world.field_help.work_speed_rate: Multiplier for how quickly Pals assigned to base work (crafting, farming, hauling) complete tasks. 1.0 is normal; higher values make base work finish faster.
world.field_help.pal_damage_rate_attack: Multiplier applied to damage dealt by Pals in combat. 1.0 is normal; higher values make Pal attacks hit harder.
world.field_help.pal_damage_rate_defense: Multiplier applied to damage received by Pals in combat. 1.0 is normal; higher values mean Pals take more damage per hit, lower values make them tankier.
world.field_help.player_damage_rate_atta: Multiplier applied to damage dealt by players in combat. 1.0 is normal; higher values make player attacks hit harder.
world.field_help.player_damage_rate_defe: Multiplier applied to damage received by players in combat. 1.0 is normal; higher values mean players take more damage per hit, lower values make them more survivable.
world.field_help.build_object_damage_rat: Multiplier for damage dealt to player-built structures by attacks. 1.0 is normal; higher values make buildings break down faster under attack.
world.field_help.build_object_deteriorat: Multiplier for the periodic decay damage applied to built objects placed outside a base camp's protected area. 1.0 is normal decay speed; higher values make unprotected structures deteriorate and collapse faster.
world.field_help.pal_stomach_decrease_ra: Multiplier for how quickly a Pal's hunger depletes. 1.0 is normal; higher values make Pals get hungry faster, lower values slow hunger loss.
world.field_help.pal_stamina_decrease_ra: Multiplier for how quickly Pals burn stamina while working, fighting, or traveling. 1.0 is normal; higher values drain stamina faster, lower values let Pals act longer before tiring.
world.field_help.pal_auto_hp_regene_rate: Multiplier for how fast Pals passively regenerate HP while active and out of combat. 1.0 is normal; higher values heal Pals faster over time.
Multiplier for how fast Pals regenerate HP while sleeping/resting. 1.0 is normal; higher values make sleeping heal Pals faster.
world.field_help.player_stomach_decrease: Multiplier for how quickly a player's hunger depletes. 1.0 is normal; higher values make players get hungry faster, lower values slow hunger loss.
world.field_help.player_stamina_decrease: Multiplier for how quickly players burn stamina while running, attacking, or gathering. 1.0 is normal; higher values drain stamina faster, lower values let players act longer before tiring.
world.field_help.player_auto_hp_regene_r: Multiplier for how fast players passively regenerate HP while active and out of combat. 1.0 is normal; higher values heal players faster over time.
Multiplier for how fast players regenerate HP while sleeping in a bed. 1.0 is normal; higher values make sleeping heal players faster.
world.field_help.build_object_hp_rate: Multiplier for the maximum HP (durability) of player-built structures. 1.0 is normal; higher values give buildings more HP, making them tougher to destroy.
world.field_help.max_building_limit_num: Caps the number of building pieces a single player may place across the world. 0 (default) means unlimited; a positive number limits each player's total structure count, which can help server performance.
world.field_help.build_area_limit: When On, prevents players from building near important points of interest (spawn points, fast-travel statues, dungeons) so those landmarks can't be blocked off or griefed with structures. Off by default (building unrestricted near those spots).
world.field_help.server_replicate_pawn_c: Distance from a player, in centimeters, beyond which the server stops replicating/tracking other Pals and characters for that client to reduce network load. Typical range 5000-15000; lower values cut server load but shrink the visible/synced radius.
world.field_help.item_container_force_ma: Technical interval, in seconds, controlling how often an open storage container's contents are force-synced/saved on the server. Default 1 second; a performance-tuning value best left alone unless many players using chests at once causes lag.
world.field_help.physics_active_drop_ite: Maximum number of dropped items that can use active physics behavior at the same time. Lower values can reduce physics load when many items are falling or moving; higher values allow more dropped items to stay physically active.
world.field_help.collection_drop_rate: Multiplier for the number of items obtained per hit when gathering from resource nodes (trees, ore, etc.). 1.0 is normal yield; higher values give more resources per gather.
world.field_help.enemy_drop_item_rate: Multiplier for the quantity of items dropped by defeated enemy Pals/creatures. 1.0 is normal; higher values increase loot dropped from kills.
world.field_help.collection_object_hp_ra: Multiplier for the 'health' of gatherable resource nodes - how many hits a tree, rock, or ore node can take before being depleted. 1.0 is normal; higher values make nodes last longer per spawn.
world.field_help.collection_object_respa: Multiplier controlling how quickly depleted resource nodes respawn. 1.0 is default speed. Multiple hosting guides note this behaves inverted from the usual convention - lower values (e.g. 0.5) make nodes respawn faster, higher values slow respawns down.
world.field_help.drop_item_max_num: Worldwide cap on the number of dropped-item objects that can exist on the ground at once (default 3000); once the cap is reached, the oldest dropped items despawn to make room for new ones.
world.field_help.drop_item_alive_max_hou: How many hours a dropped item stays on the ground before automatically despawning. Higher values let dropped loot persist longer; lower values clean up dropped items faster.
world.field_help.supply_drop_span: Minutes between periodic supply-drop/meteorite events that deliver loot crates to the map. Lower values make drops occur more frequently, higher values make them rarer.
world.field_help.active_unko: When On, Pals periodically produce 'UNKO' (poop) items that can be collected and used as fertilizer. Off by default (Pals do not produce poop items).
world.field_help.drop_item_max_num_unko: Separate worldwide cap (like DropItemMaxNum) specifically for dropped Pal poop (UNKO) items; once exceeded, the oldest poop items despawn.
world.field_help.equipment_durability_da: Multiplier for how fast equipped gear (weapons, tools, armor) loses durability with use. 1.0 is normal wear speed; higher values make equipment wear out and need repair faster.
world.field_help.item_corruption_multipl: Multiplier for how quickly perishable items (mainly food) spoil/decay in inventory and storage. 1.0 is normal decay speed; values above 1 make food spoil faster, values below 1 make it last longer.
world.field_help.deny_technology_list: Comma-separated list of Technology (crafting recipe) IDs that players are permanently blocked from unlocking - commonly used to ban PvP-affecting items like the Grappling Gun or Guild Chest.
world.field_help.item_weight_rate: Multiplier for how much items weigh in a player's inventory. 1.0 is normal; lower values make items weigh less (allowing more to be carried), higher values make them heavier.
world.field_help.death_penalty: Determines what a player loses on death. None: nothing is lost. Item: only inventory items are dropped (equipped gear kept). ItemAndEquipment: inventory items and equipped gear are both dropped. All: inventory items, equipped gear, and party/inventory Pals are all dropped and lootable by others.
world.field_help.enable_aim_assist_pad: Toggles server-side aim assist for players using gamepads/controllers. On by default; turning it Off disables controller aim assist for all players.
world.field_help.enable_aim_assist_keybo: Toggles server-side aim assist for mouse-and-keyboard players. Off by default; often left off on PvP servers to keep aiming skill-based.
world.field_help.character_recreate_in_h: In Hardcore mode, controls whether a player whose character has permanently died is allowed to create a brand-new character to keep playing, rather than being locked out entirely.
world.field_help.is_pvp: Master switch for player-vs-player combat. When Off (default), players generally cannot fight each other; when On, PvP damage becomes possible (still gated by bEnablePlayerToPlayerDamage).
world.field_help.enable_player_to_player: When On, players can deal damage directly to other players. When Off (default), players cannot hurt each other even if bIsPvP is enabled.
world.field_help.enable_defense_other_gu: When On, automated base defenses (turrets, guard Pals) will attack trespassing players from other guilds. Off by default, meaning base defenses ignore players from rival guilds.
world.field_help.enable_invader_enemy: When On (default), hostile 'invader' enemies can randomly raid and attack player bases. When Off, these base-invasion raid attacks are disabled.
world.field_help.hardcore: Enables Hardcore mode: when On, player death is permanent rather than a simple respawn. Off by default, meaning death only applies the normal death penalty and the player respawns.
world.field_help.pal_lost: When On, any Pals in the player's party are permanently lost upon that player's death. Off by default, meaning Pals are not permanently lost when the owner dies.
world.field_help.enable_friendly_fire: When On, players in the same guild/party can damage each other. Off by default, meaning guildmates cannot hurt one another even with PvP enabled.
world.field_help.can_pickup_other_guild_: When On, players can loot the item drops left behind when a player from a different guild dies. Off by default, meaning only the deceased player's own guildmates can pick it up.
world.field_help.additional_drop_item_wh: When On, killing another player in PvP causes a bonus item (set by AdditionalDropItemWhenPlayerKillingInPvPMode) to drop in addition to the normal death-penalty items. Off by default.
world.field_help.additional_drop_item_nu: Quantity of the bonus item dropped per PvP kill when bAdditionalDropItemWhenPlayerKillingInPvPMode is enabled. Default 1.
world.field_help.additional_drop_item_wh: The item ID dropped as a bonus reward when one player kills another in PvP mode, used together with the enable toggle and quantity setting for this feature.
world.field_help.display_pvp_item_num_on: When On, shows a count of PvP-related bonus items currently held/dropped by players as markers on the world map - used by PvP servers that track item-based objectives.
When On, shows a count of PvP-related bonus items currently stored at base camps as markers on the world map, mirroring the player-item display for base camps.
world.field_help.invisible_other_guild_b: When On, hides the visual boundary effect that normally marks the edge of other guilds' base camps, so rival bases aren't obvious at a glance. Off by default (other guilds' base areas are visible).
world.field_help.respawn_penalty_duratio: Part of the escalating respawn-penalty system: if a player survives this many seconds without dying again, their respawn delay resets back to the base BlockRespawnTime instead of continuing to escalate.
world.field_help.respawn_penalty_time_sc: Part of the escalating respawn-penalty system: each death that occurs before RespawnPenaltyDurationThreshold resets the timer multiplies the respawn wait by this scale - e.g. 2.0 doubles the delay with every consecutive death.
world.field_help.block_respawn_time: Base number of seconds a player must wait after dying before being allowed to respawn - the starting delay that RespawnPenaltyTimeScale escalates on repeated deaths.
world.field_help.base_camp_max_num: Worldwide cap on the total number of base camps that can exist on the map at once, counting all guilds combined. Default 128.
world.field_help.base_camp_worker_max_nu: Maximum number of Pals that can be assigned to work at a single base camp at once. Default 15; raising it significantly can hurt server performance.
world.field_help.base_camp_max_num_in_gu: Maximum number of separate base camps a single guild is allowed to own at once. Default 4.
world.field_help.guild_player_max_num: Maximum number of players allowed to be members of a single guild. Default 20.
world.field_help.coop_player_max_num: Maximum party size for non-dedicated 'join via Steam' co-op sessions (as opposed to a dedicated server). Default 4; reported to have limited effect on true dedicated servers.
world.field_help.server_player_max_num: Maximum number of players who can be connected to the dedicated server at the same time. Hard cap is 32.
world.field_help.auto_reset_guild_no_onl: When On, guilds are automatically disbanded once none of their members have logged in for the time set by AutoResetGuildTimeNoOnlinePlayers. Off by default.
world.field_help.auto_reset_guild_time_n: Number of hours a guild can have zero members online before it is automatically disbanded, when bAutoResetGuildNoOnlinePlayers is enabled. Default 72 hours.
world.field_help.guild_rejoin_cooldown_m: Minutes a player must wait after leaving a guild before they are allowed to join or rejoin a guild again. Default 0 (no cooldown).
world.field_help.pal_egg_default_hatchin: Real-time hours it takes the largest/longest-incubating egg tier to hatch; smaller egg tiers hatch proportionally faster, scaled down from this value. Default 72 hours; 0 makes eggs hatch instantly.
world.field_help.allow_enhance_stat_heal: When On (default), players can spend enhancement points at Statues of Power to increase their max Health stat. When Off, that stat cannot be enhanced.
world.field_help.allow_enhance_stat_stam: When On (default), players can spend enhancement points at Statues of Power to increase their max Stamina stat. When Off, that stat cannot be enhanced.
world.field_help.allow_enhance_stat_atta: When On (default), players can spend enhancement points at Statues of Power to increase their Attack stat. When Off, that stat cannot be enhanced - often disabled on PvP servers to limit power creep.
world.field_help.allow_enhance_stat_weig: When On (default), players can spend enhancement points at Statues of Power to increase their carry-Weight stat. When Off, that stat cannot be enhanced.
world.field_help.allow_enhance_stat_work: When On (default), players can spend enhancement points at Statues of Power to increase their Work Speed stat. When Off, that stat cannot be enhanced.
world.field_help.is_start_location_selec: When On (default), new players can choose their starting location on the map when beginning the game instead of always spawning at a fixed point.
world.field_help.enable_non_login_penalt: When On (default), Pals belonging to a player who hasn't logged in for a long time can suffer a condition penalty (e.g. becoming unhappy/less effective) while that player is away. Off removes the inactivity penalty.
world.field_help.enable_fast_travel_only: When On, fast travel is restricted to your own base camps only (other fast-travel points can't be used), often paired with PvP rules to stop players teleporting away from a fight. Off by default.
world.field_help.enable_fast_travel: When On (default), players can fast-travel between unlocked Fast Travel points/statues and bases. When Off, fast travel is disabled entirely.
world.field_help.exist_player_after_logo: When On, a player's character remains physically present in the world (lying down/sleeping) after logging out instead of disappearing, so logging out can't be used to escape a fight. Off by default.
world.field_help.enable_predator_boss_pa: When On (default), rare roaming 'Predator' boss-tier Pals can spawn in the world, offering tough fights with better loot. When Off, these boss Pal encounters do not spawn.
world.field_help.allow_global_palbox_exp: When On (default), players can export Pals from this server into their account-wide Global Palbox (cross-server Pal storage). When Off, exporting Pals out of this world is disabled.
world.field_help.allow_global_palbox_imp: When On, players can import Pals from their account-wide Global Palbox into this server. Off by default - many servers keep this disabled to stop players bringing in overleveled Pals from other worlds.
world.field_help.server_name: The name of the server as shown to players in the in-game server browser.
world.field_help.server_description: A short description shown under the server name in the server browser - often used for rates, rules, or a Discord link.
world.field_help.server_password: Password required for a player to join the server. Leave blank for an open server anyone can join.
world.field_help.admin_password: Password required to gain in-game admin privileges (admin chat commands) and to authenticate RCON/REST API connections.
world.field_help.public_port: The network port the server listens on and advertises publicly for players to connect to. Default 8211.
world.field_help.public_ip: Manually specifies the public IP address the server advertises to players/the server list, instead of letting it auto-detect.
world.field_help.rcon_enabled: Turns on the RCON remote console interface, letting external tools (admin panels, Discord bots) send server commands remotely. Off by default.
world.field_help.rcon_port: The TCP port used for RCON remote console connections when RCONEnabled is On. Default 25575.
world.field_help.restapi_enabled: Turns on the server's REST (HTTP) admin API, which Palsitter uses for management features. On by default.
world.field_help.restapi_port: The TCP port used for the REST API when RESTAPIEnabled is On. Default 8212.
world.field_help.enable_gamedata_api: Adds -enable-gamedata-api to the PalServer launch command, enabling the REST /game-data world actor snapshot endpoint. On by default.
world.field_help.crossplay_platforms: Select the platforms allowed to connect: Steam, Xbox, PS5, and Mac. Clearing a platform blocks players on that platform from joining.
world.field_help.region: A free-text region label for the server, shown in the community server browser to help players find nearby servers.
world.field_help.use_auth: When On (default), the server requires players to authenticate (e.g. via Steam) before joining, helping prevent impersonation/ID spoofing. Off removes that authentication check.
world.field_help.ban_list_url: The URL the server polls to download its list of banned player IDs. Defaults to Palworld's official ban list; can be pointed at a custom list instead.
world.field_help.allow_client_mod: When On (default), players running client-side mods are allowed to connect to the server. When Off, modded clients are blocked from joining.
world.field_help.is_multiplay: Legacy multiplayer toggle originally meant to allow other players into a hosted world; on true dedicated servers (which are inherently multiplayer) this flag is widely reported to have no effect.
world.field_help.show_player_list: When On, enables displaying the list of currently connected players for a dedicated server (e.g. in the in-game player list). Off by default.
world.field_help.is_show_join_left_messa: When On (default), a chat message announces when a player joins or leaves the server. When Off, these notifications are suppressed.
world.field_help.chat_post_limit_per_min: Maximum number of chat messages a single player can post per minute, used to prevent chat spam/flooding. Default 30.
world.field_help.auto_save_span: Interval, in seconds, between automatic saves of the world to disk. Lower values save more often (less data-loss risk, more disk/CPU use); higher values save less often.
world.field_help.is_use_backup_save_data: When On (default), the server keeps rolling backup copies of save data, useful for recovering from corruption. When Off, no automatic backups are kept.
world.field_help.log_format_type: Controls the format of server log output. Text writes human-readable log lines; Json writes structured JSON log entries, which is easier for log-parsing/monitoring tools to consume.
startup.conflictDetail: Would you like Palsitter to stop the process using this port?
startup.alternateMessage: Palsitter could not start on port {port}.
startup.alternateDetail: Would you like Palsitter to choose another available port?
add.title: Add server
add.no_dedicated_worlds: No dedicated worlds containing Level.sav were found.
add.scan_first: Scan the current source path and select a world before creating the instance.
add.select_level_save: Select a valid Level.sav file before creating the instance.
add.invalid_import_layout: The selected Level.sav is not inside a recognized Palworld world folder. Select Level.sav from a folder named with its 32-character world ID, including the companion files.
add.single_player_message: Single-player world imported. World data is preserved, but the player identity may need migration before the original character can be used on the dedicated server.
add.coop_message: Co-op world imported. Player identities may need migration before the original characters can be used on the dedicated server.
add.error_exists: A server profile with that name already exists.
add.error_invalid: Use letters, numbers, hyphens, or underscores, and do not start the name with template.
add.error_source: The selected source profile is unavailable.
add.error_unknown: Could not create the server profile: {error}
satisfactory.title: Satisfactory
satisfactory.not_implemented: Satisfactory server support is not implemented yet. This instance is a placeholder.
